comments.text: AWRI summer interns have been hitting Pere Marquette beach with the Laker Raker BeBot drone! In addition to keeping microplastics out of our Great Lakes, interns will sort and catalogue litter for microplastics research. #Meijer #GLPCleanup #CGLRGreatLakes #PollutionProbe
How much of a problem are the microplastics in our waterways, especially Lake Ontario? The Weather Network's Victoria Fenn Alvarado spoke to an expert to find out.

Oh look, microplastics are ubiquitous in all environmental media (e.g., water, sediment, biota, and beaches) in the Great Lakes basin, and they are especially concentrated in more populated systems such as Lakes Michigan and Ontario. #collapse

Don't miss out on all the fantastic research featured in the first 2026 issue of the Journal of Great Lakes Research, with studies on microplastic hotspots, invasive impacts, walleye behavior, and more. JGLR, vol. 52, issue 1: #JGLR #GreatLakesSci

Don't miss out on the latest issue of the Journal of Great Lakes Research, featuring a special section on Great Lakes coastal processes and articles on microcystin persistence, PCBs, microplastics, and more. JGLR, vol. 51, issue 4: #JGLR #GreatLakesSci

Everything to know about microplastics in the Great Lakes By Lisa John Rogers, Great Lakes Now Shortly after Chelsea Rochman started her faculty job at the University of Toronto, she began working with the Government of Canada to sample microplastics in fish from Lake Ontario. What she found, she…

In my final Ecology Center article, I examine why labels like “#1 plastic” or “100% recycled” are misleading. Drawing on new research, I show that both virgin and recycled PET can leach hormone-disrupting chemicals, raising serious concerns for public health and the Great Lakes.

Come work with us! We are hiring a Post-doctoral Researcher for the Lake Ontario Center for Microplastic and Human Health. Research will focus on input, transport, and fate of plastic debris in the Great Lakes basin. The position will begin ASAP.
For additional info, job #9770BR at rit.edu/careers.
Donna Kashian, Tim Hoellein, and Rebecca Rooney are 3 scientists I trust when it comes to microplastics and water quality in the Great Lakes!

We joined our colleagues from .bsky.social .bsky.social Lake Ontario MicroPlastics Center on a tour of Monroe County's EcoPark to learn how they recycle vape devices. Vapes contain hazardous waste such as lithium-ion batteries, heavy metals, and liquid nicotine. #vapesaretrash

Every fish sampled in Lake Ontario contained microplastics, averaging around 100 pieces per gut. That’s far worse than what researchers found in the Pacific Garbage Patch. #Microplastics #PlasticPollution #RethinkPlastic

Great Lakes microplastics research could inform national and global policy Read the full story at Inside Climate News. Microplastics are increasingly present in samples pulled from the Great Lakes. Researchers are working to understand them—and to remove them.
Great Lakes Summit calls for more microplastics research, monitoring
Scientists studying the Great Lakes continued their research that could pinpoint how microplastics impact human health and ultimately inform decisions made across the world.
Studies are sketchy, but it appears Lake Ontario has the most microplastics per volume in the Great Lakes.

Save the Date! Great Lakes Microplastics Research Symposium This all-day event on June 18 will bring together experts, trainees, and community members to discuss emerging findings on the relationship between microplastics and health in the Great Lakes region. Abstract submission opens Feb. 9!
